reason: The item cleanly targets pathway-dependent thermal runaway metrics (T1 and onset-to-TR interval) and forces mechanism-aware interpretation (e.g., lithium plating vs high-temperature storage modes) rather than SOH-only reasoning. Decoys are largely single-fault and technically plausible, especially the SOH-equivalence trap and the T3→energy inference trap. Minor neutrality risk comes from some options being more elaborated/mechanistic than others, which can weakly cue correctness, but overall discrimination remains strong for experts.

The prompt cleanly targets pathway-dependent thermal runaway behavior at matched SOH and constrains the explanation to dominant aging mechanisms and their anode–electrolyte reactivity implications, with an explicit requirement to rule out cathode-driven differentiation. It has strong discriminative value because a correct response must connect lithium plating vs SEI-dominated aging to shifts in onset temperature and runaway acceleration, not just restate “SOH differs from safety.” The main quality limitation is that as a short-answer item it lacks decoy options, so single-fault decoy craftsmanship cannot be fully exercised and some boundary wording (e.g., “governing” and “not expected”) still admits mild gray-zone reasoning.
